mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower recession risk, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and higher recession odds, which depresses risk appetite and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST is also expanding, it signals broad final-demand strength and inventory turnover support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform each single series alone, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 52% of jointly strong observations over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and slower corporate revenue momentum, which should pressure earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after 2 consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy is accommodative and equities should gain over the next 20 trading days as discount rates stay restrained.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread condition does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if returns become systematically negative when the spread is wide.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in PAYEMS together with falling UNRATE signals improving labor income and consumption capacity, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns fail to exceed baseline after simultaneous PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines, or if the joint signal underperforms either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST from an already weak level indicates collapsing residential demand and future activity spillovers, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE marks labor normalization that reduces default and demand risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after labor normalization episodes, or if the signal ceases to work once the economy is outside recovery windows.
